title: [ipv6_addr.c:75] Hash buffer not explicitly sized to TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE
description:
Gap: lichen_pubkey_to_iid declares its hash buffer size but relies on TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE being 32.

What: Line 75 declares 'uint8_t hash[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE];' but there is no compile-time assertion that TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE == 32. While lichen_util.c has this BUILD_ASSERT, ipv6_addr.c compiles independently (especially in host tests) and could silently break if TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE changed.

Why it matters: Host-side unit tests may use different TinyCrypt headers. If the constant drifted, memcpy(iid, hash, 8) could read from a buffer of unexpected size. The build would succeed but produce incorrect IIDs. Add BUILD_ASSERT(TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE == 32) in ipv6_addr.c.
close_reason: Verified: hash buffer at line 75 already uses TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE (uint8_t hash[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE]). Compile-time assertions at lines 212-217 verify buffer sizing. No change needed.
